vulnerability.reference: Intermediate representations from user-uploaded photos retain enough identity and scene detail to be inverted into recognizable private images.
vulnerability.generated: Split inference transmits intermediate representations retaining rich visual structure, and deployed cloud vision models lack defenses against guided diffusion reconstruction.

impact.reference: Recovered personal photos expose intimate moments, enabling blackmail and identity theft.
impact.generated: Private user photos are reconstructed with high fidelity, exposing intimate imagery and enabling identity theft, blackmail, or unauthorized profiling.

dose_response.reference: Identity and scene detail in intermediate representations enable inversion of user-uploaded photos, exposing intimate moments and sensitive documents, which leads to blackmail, identity theft, and child safety liabilities.
dose_response.generated: Intermediate representations leak visual data; through user photo uploads to cloud APIs, this triggers transmission of structurally rich representations and lack of defenses, leading to high-fidelity reconstruction of private photos exposing intimate imagery and enabling identity theft, blackmail, or profiling.
